title: Release of the NULIPA-SACE general assembly schedule
text: The official schedule for the NULIPA-SACE general assembly is now available. Faculty members, student leaders, and students are advised to review the complete program, attendance instructions, and venue assignments.

audience: Faculty, Program Chairs, and Student Officers
title: Deadline for submission of official notices to the NULIPA-SACE board
text: All units submitting school-wide announcements are reminded to forward approved bulletin materials on or before Friday to ensure timely posting on the NULIPA-SACE academic bulletin board.

title: Call for participation in the inter-program academic innovation challenge
text: Students from Architecture, BS Computer Science, BS Information Technology, Multimedia Arts, Computer Engineering, and Engineering are invited to participate in the inter-program academic innovation challenge. Submission guidelines and timelines are available through the bulletin board.

title: Display schedule for third-year studio output
text: The School of Architecture has posted the official display schedule for third-year studio output. Faculty and students may view the exhibited models and presentation boards during the announced gallery hours.

title: Reassignment of rooms for midterm jury sessions
text: Selected Architecture design sections have updated room assignments for midterm jury sessions. Students and faculty panelists are advised to review the final venue list before the scheduled presentation period.

title: Registration for the parametric facade workshop
text: Registration is now open for the parametric facade workshop. The session will cover Rhino and Grasshopper workflows for environmental analysis and academic design presentation requirements.

title: Academic coding activity for BS Computer Science students
text: BS Computer Science students are advised of the scheduled academic coding activity this Saturday. The program includes mentor checkpoints, code review sessions, and final presentations of student outputs.

audience: BS Computer Science Faculty and Students
title: Software laboratory server maintenance advisory
text: The software laboratory development server will undergo maintenance from 7:00 PM to 10:00 PM. Faculty and students are advised to save files in advance and avoid system deployment during the maintenance period.

title: Publication of capstone research consultation schedules
text: Faculty consultation schedules for machine learning, data science, systems, and software engineering capstone groups have been posted. Students are advised to review available time slots and coordinate accordingly.

title: Laboratory and workshop PPE compliance advisory
text: All Engineering laboratories and workshop spaces will conduct personal protective equipment compliance checks before afternoon sessions. Faculty and students are reminded to observe safety requirements at all times.

title: Schedule of Engineering capstone demonstration trials
text: The schedule for Engineering capstone demonstration trials has been posted. Faculty and students may attend the presentation of project progress, subsystem updates, and testing results during the designated demo window.

title: Additional booking schedule for the materials testing laboratory
text: Additional morning booking schedules are now available for concrete, metals, fabrication, and stress analysis sessions in the materials testing laboratory.

title: Announcement of digital portfolio review week
text: Multimedia Arts students may now sign up for faculty review slots covering branding, motion graphics, illustration, and interactive media portfolio requirements.

title: Schedule for the motion graphics academic workshop
text: The workshop schedule for motion graphics, animation timing, title design, and short-form visual storytelling has been posted for Multimedia Arts faculty and students.

title: Deadline for submission of exhibition requirements
text: Graduating Multimedia Arts students are reminded to submit final display boards, reels, captions, and artist statements before the official exhibit curation deadline.

title: Schedule of embedded systems project demonstrations
text: Computer Engineering student teams will present embedded systems projects and prototype outputs for faculty evaluation. Demonstrations include hardware performance, documentation, and systems integration review.

title: Updated release schedule for hardware laboratory components
text: The updated borrowing and return schedule for boards, sensors, kits, and testing equipment used in Computer Engineering laboratory classes is now available for faculty and students.

title: Opening of capstone integration consultation schedules
text: Computer Engineering senior groups may now reserve consultation schedules for hardware-software integration, testing procedures, and final technical presentation review.
